officialEvidence.finding: Hyprland exposes --config FILE/-c FILE and --verify-config. The help text for --verify-config says it does not run Hyprland and only prints whether the config has errors.
officialEvidence.implication: A future harness can likely validate a generated temporary config without mutating the active user config or active compositor. Exact local command shape still needs implementation-time verification.

officialEvidence.finding: Hyprland selects an explicit config path first, then HYPRLAND_CONFIG, then normal user config locations.
officialEvidence.implication: A temp config file can be supplied explicitly or through HYPRLAND_CONFIG for isolated validation.

officialEvidence.finding: hyprctl eval is only supported with the Lua config manager and calls the Lua config manager eval path.
officialEvidence.implication: eval is not a pure key/value config validation path and is incompatible with this app safety rule forbidding Lua execution/evaluation.

officialEvidence.finding: CConfigManager::eval loads and executes Lua code with luaL_loadstring and guarded pcall.
officialEvidence.implication: eval executes Lua and may mutate runtime state; it must not be used by this app as a validation shortcut.

officialEvidence.finding: hyprctl keyword emitted the non-legacy parser message for sampled rows: keyword cannot work with non-legacy parsers and suggests eval. configerrors stayed clean and getoption stayed unchanged.
officialEvidence.implication: Active-session keyword probing is not a reliable acceptance proof for the current configuration provider.

candidateApproaches.evidence: The existing harness can roll back runtime probes, but keyword is not accepted by non-legacy parser sessions and getoption remains unchanged.
candidateApproaches.conclusion: Keep only as a rollback-protected live-observability probe, not as config-persistence proof.

candidateApproaches.evidence: Official source shows eval is Lua-manager-only and executes Lua code.
candidateApproaches.conclusion: Do not use in this app because Lua execution/evaluation is forbidden.

candidateApproaches.evidence: reload is explicitly disallowed for this sprint and would affect the active compositor session.
The app can generate temp configs and prove parser/write/reread safety without touching user config.
candidateApproaches.conclusion: Required for every candidate, but not enough by itself to promote accepted-unobservable rows.

candidateApproaches.evidence: Official source advertises --verify-config as not running Hyprland and only printing config errors; --config supplies an alternate config file.
candidateApproaches.conclusion: Preferred proof path for config-valid but non-live-observable scalar rows.

candidateApproaches.evidence: Could validate a real compositor process but may require display/session setup and more cleanup risk.
candidateApproaches.conclusion: Use only if --verify-config is insufficient and after dedicated approval.

recommendedApproach.summary: Validate parser roundtrip and writer roundtrip against temp files, then validate the generated temp config with Hyprland --verify-config using an explicit temp config path. Do not mutate active config or active runtime.
minimumProofForFutureEnablement: row remains readable by Rust parser
fixture scalar writer can replace and append safely
generated temp config includes only the intended candidate mutation
Hyprland --verify-config against the temp config exits successfully with no config errors
coverage report and tests are updated in the later implementation sprint
notEnoughForEnablement: hyprctl keyword process exit success by itself
configerrors staying clean after keyword with unchanged getoption
parser and writer roundtrip without Hyprland config verification
manual visual observation without machine-readable validation

unsafeApproaches: hyprctl eval for validation because it executes Lua
ad hoc hyprctl keyword without rollback watchdog
starting nested compositor without a dedicated safety design
deferredApproaches: Batch B/C/D/E/G live or persistence validation
requiredImplementationStages: Create temp directory and temp config generation module.
Apply each candidate setting to temp config only through existing parser/writer paths.
Verify parser and writer roundtrip against temp config.
Locate Hyprland binary and verify --verify-config/--config support with help output only.
Run Hyprland --verify-config with explicit temp config path under timeout and capture stdout/stderr/exit status.
Only in a later sprint, promote rows that pass the full proof policy.
safetyRequirements: No active runtime keyword probing for config-persistence proof.
All temp files must be outside the repo or under ignored temp paths and cleaned up unless explicitly preserving a failure fixture.
Treat any config error output as a failed proof.
openQuestions: Confirm exact local Hyprland command ordering: Hyprland --verify-config --config <temp-file> versus Hyprland --config <temp-file> --verify-config.
Confirm whether --verify-config requires compositor environment variables on this system despite official non-run semantics.
Define whether a minimal temp config needs autogenerated boilerplate beyond the candidate setting.
Decide whether successful config-persistence proof is enough for Batch A promotion or whether user wants manual observation first.
